results.question: How should I design memory for a production multi-agent system?
relevant_tools: LangChain (ecosystem agility and composability)
Deep Agents (planning + filesystem + shell execution framework)
Vector databases for semantic search and long-term knowledge retrieval
Ontology/knowledge graph systems for multi-agent coordination
results.recommendation_snippet: Implement a tiered memory architecture with short-term context buffers (session/conversation state), long-term persistent storage (knowledge base), and a semantic ontology layer for multi-agent coordi

results.question: Should I use RAG or fine-tuning for domain adaptation in a production LLM system?
relevant_tools: llmfit (hardware compatibility scanner for model selection)
Chunking strategy frameworks (11 documented approaches per Srinivasan)
LLM-as-Judge for evaluation, with caveats about process integration
results.recommendation_snippet: For production domain adaptation, prefer RAG as your primary strategy—it offers faster iteration, lower computational cost, and safer deployment without model retraining. Use fine-tuning only when RAG

results.question: What are the best tools for agentic coding workflows in 2025?
relevant_tools: Claude Code (native agentic orchestration platform)
Claude Code best-practices repository (117k+ GitHub stars, 13 agents, 56+ skills, 32 commands)
Deep Agents (LangChain open-source, MIT licensed alternative)
MCP servers: Context7, Playwright, DeepWiki
Git Worktrees (parallel development)
Cursor IDE, OpenCode, Codex CLI (alternative interfaces)
results.recommendation_snippet: For agentic coding workflows in 2025, adopt Claude Code as your primary platform—it has proven production maturity (10 months daily use, 992 internal tests, real shipped products) and native multi-age

results.question: How should I approach evaluation for an agentic AI system I'm building?
relevant_tools: Adversarial Dev (GAN-inspired three-agent harness with separate planning, building, evaluation agents)
Claude Agent SDK or Codex SDK (platform-agnostic agent implementation)
Genetic-Pareto feedback loops with logs-as-actionable-intel for skill optimization
Weights & Biases (LLM-as-a-judge infrastructure for automated evaluation)
results.recommendation_snippet: Implement a multi-agent adversarial evaluation architecture that separates planning, building, and evaluation into distinct agents with opposing incentives, rather than relying on self-evaluation. Pai

results.question: What architectural patterns should I use for orchestrating multiple specialized AI agents?
relevant_tools: Claude Agent SDK / Codex SDK (for multi-agent harness patterns)
Pydantic AI, LangChain, n8n (agent frameworks, though note mainstream focus has shifted)
Archon (open-source harness builder for orchestration and determinism)
Palantir-style 12-layer stack (reference architecture for ontology, context engineering, and permissions)
results.recommendation_snippet: Adopt a harness-based orchestration pattern that separates concerns (planning, building, evaluation) into distinct specialized agents with adversarial tension to drive quality. Layer LLM integration,
